topic: Embeddings and the adaptation spectrum: from frozen extraction to fine-tuning
scope: Brief recap of static vs. contextual (defined in Ch 11), then the adaptation continuum: frozen extractor -> partial -> full fine-tuning, with trade-offs.
key_concepts: Recap of embeddings: vector representations that capture semantic meaning, transitioning from static to contextual.
The adaptation spectrum: a continuum defining how much of a pretrained model's weights are modified for a new task.
Frozen extraction: using the model as a fixed feature extractor without updating its weights. Fast, cheap, and robust against overfitting on small datasets.
Partial fine-tuning: unfreezing only the final layers of the model, allowing adaptation to specific tasks while preserving generalized foundational features.
Full fine-tuning: updating all weights in the model. Highly expressive but computationally expensive, requiring large data and risking catastrophic forgetting.

definitions.definition: A pretrained model used strictly to compute feature vectors, with its internal weights locked during training.
The process of further training a pretrained model's weights on a specific downstream dataset.

examples: Extracting image embeddings using a frozen ResNet, then training a simple logistic regression on those embeddings.
Performing full fine-tuning on a BERT model for a highly specialized legal text classification task.

relations.precedes: ch15_s02 (how to store and measure similarity between extracted embeddings).
relations.follows: Chapter 11 (which introduced static vs contextual text embeddings) and Chapter 12 (pretrained image/audio models).

figures.caption: Spektrum adaptasi model pretrained
figures.description: Diagram menunjukkan kontinum dari frozen extractor, partial fine-tuning, hingga full fine-tuning. Menyoroti layer mana yang dikunci (beku) dan layer mana yang diperbarui (dilatih).

exclusions: Do not detail the internal architecture of Transformers or CNNs (already covered in previous chapters).
